vandie2012.author: van Die, M.D., Burger, H.G., Teede, H.J. and Bone, K.M.
vandie2012.title: Vitex agnus-castus extracts for female reproductive disorders: a systematic review of clinical trials

daniele2005.author: Daniele, C., Thompson Coon, J., Pittler, M.H. and Ernst, E.
daniele2005.title: Vitex agnus castus: a systematic review of adverse events

puglia2023.author: Puglia, L.T., Lowry, J. and Tamagno, G.
puglia2023.title: Vitex agnus castus effects on hyperprolactinaemia

actions.action: Eases cyclical breast pain (mastalgia) and menstrual-cycle complaints including cramping
Lowers mildly raised prolactin and normalises a short luteal phase (dopaminergic D2 action)
Supports menstrual irregularities and perimenopausal cycle symptoms (traditional / Commission E)

constituents.name: Diterpenes (e.g. rotundifuran, clerodadienols)
constituents.note: Bind dopamine D2 receptors in the pituitary, reducing prolactin release - the key mechanism.

contraindications.note: Reported adverse events are mild and reversible (nausea, headache, gastrointestinal upset, menstrual changes, acne, itching or rash).

Because it acts on dopamine and the hormonal axis, avoid alongside dopamine-antagonist (antipsychotic) or dopamine-agonist drugs and use caution with hormonal medicines (oral contraceptives, HRT) and hormone-sensitive conditions.

internal_notes: Enriched 2026-06-12 from scientific literature (Planta Medica efficacy review; Drug Safety adverse-events review; Frontiers in Endocrinology prolactin review). Vitex is now placed in Lamiaceae (formerly Verbenaceae). Originally a skeleton imported from the omniasana.bio Symptom-to-Plant Lookup tool (pms, menstrual-cramps, menopause, acne); associations retained and now part-mapped and cited.

hasanudin2012.author: Hasanudin, K., Hashim, P. and Mustafa, S.
hasanudin2012.title: Corn Silk (Stigma Maydis) in Healthcare: A Phytochemical and Pharmacological Review

actions.action: Diuretic - increases urine output to support the urinary tract and mild fluid retention/swelling
Soothing demulcent for urinary-tract irritation and mild urinary infections; supports kidney function

constituents.name: Flavonoids (including maysin)
constituents.note: Major antioxidant constituents of corn silk.

constituents.name: Phenolic compounds
constituents.note: Contribute to antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activity.

constituents.name: Potassium salts, polysaccharides and saponins
constituents.note: Associated with the diuretic and demulcent actions.

contraindications.note: As a diuretic it should be taken with plenty of fluids; use caution alongside prescription diuretics or medicines that affect potassium.
May lower blood sugar and blood pressure, so use caution with antidiabetic or antihypertensive medication; avoid concentrated medicinal doses in pregnancy.

internal_notes: Enriched 2026-06-12 from scientific literature (Molecules phytochemical and pharmacological review). Originally a skeleton imported from the omniasana.bio Symptom-to-Plant Lookup tool (uti, swelling, kidney-support); associations retained and now part-mapped and cited.

ema.author: European Medicines Agency (HMPC)
ema.title: European Union herbal monograph on Agropyron repens (L.) P. Beauv., rhizoma (Agropyri repentis rhizoma)

actions.action: Diuretic irrigation of the urinary tract - adjuvant in minor urinary complaints (taken with plenty of fluids)

constituents.name: Polysaccharides (the fructan triticin) and mucilage
constituents.note: Soothing, demulcent carbohydrates of the rhizome.

constituents.name: Flavonoids and phenolic acids (caffeoyl/feruloyl quinic esters)

contraindications.note: Drink plenty of fluids during use. Irrigation therapy must NOT be used if there is oedema (swelling) due to impaired heart or kidney function.
Traditional use only; if urinary symptoms persist or are accompanied by fever, spasms or blood in the urine, seek medical care. Use in pregnancy and breastfeeding is not recommended.

internal_notes: Enriched 2026-06-12 from regulatory and phytotherapy literature (EMA HMPC monograph; ESCOP monograph). Elymus repens = Agropyron repens = Elytrigia repens. Originally a skeleton imported from the omniasana.bio Symptom-to-Plant Lookup tool (uti, swelling, kidney-support); associations retained and now part-mapped and cited.

herbalreality.author: Herbal Reality
herbalreality.title: Cramp Bark (Viburnum opulus): Benefits, Uses, Safety

actions.action: Antispasmodic for menstrual cramps (relaxes uterine smooth muscle)
Antispasmodic for general muscle spasm and cramping (smooth-muscle relaxant)

constituents.name: Coumarins (scopoletin)
constituents.note: A principal antispasmodic constituent of the bark.

constituents.name: Viopudial and hydroquinones (arbutin)
constituents.note: Contribute to the smooth-muscle-relaxant and astringent activity.

contraindications.note: Only the bark is used medicinally; the raw berries are toxic and cause stomach upset, vomiting and diarrhoea if eaten in quantity.
Human safety data are limited; medicinal use in pregnancy or breastfeeding should only be under the guidance of a qualified practitioner.

internal_notes: Enriched 2026-06-12 from general phytotherapy literature (Herbal Reality monograph; ScienceDirect overview). Viburnum opulus is poorly covered by clinical trials, so some actions remain needs-review. Originally a skeleton imported from the omniasana.bio Symptom-to-Plant Lookup tool (menstrual-cramps, muscle-spasm, pain); associations retained and now part-mapped and cited.

ema.author: European Medicines Agency (HMPC)
ema.title: European Union herbal monograph on Verbascum thapsus L., V. densiflorum Bertol. and V. phlomoides L., flos (Verbasci flos)

actions.action: Demulcent and mild expectorant for cough and cold with sore throat and dry, irritating cough
Soothes catarrh of the upper respiratory tract / bronchitis (mucilage coats irritated mucosa; saponins loosen mucus)

constituents.name: Mucilage polysaccharides (~3%)
constituents.note: The demulcent constituents that soothe mucous membranes.

constituents.name: Saponins
constituents.note: Thought to be responsible for the mild expectorant action.

contraindications.note: Per the EU herbal monograph, for use in adults and adolescents over 12 years. Cough that persists beyond about a week, or is accompanied by fever, shortness of breath or purulent sputum, needs medical assessment.
Use in pregnancy and breastfeeding is not recommended. Strain liquid preparations to remove the fine flower/leaf hairs, which can irritate the throat.

internal_notes: Enriched 2026-06-12 from regulatory and phytotherapy literature (EMA HMPC monograph and ESCOP monograph, which cover V. densiflorum together with V. thapsus and V. phlomoides). Originally a skeleton imported from the omniasana.bio Symptom-to-Plant Lookup tool (cough, bronchitis, earache, sore-throat); associations retained and now part-mapped and cited.
